./docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md:33:Agentic AI compresses what used to be person-weeks of engineering into hours. The bottleneck moves: writing code is no longer the binding constraint — keeping intent, artifacts, and evidence coherent across many parallel agent invocations is. Teams that hit this new ceiling keep hitting the same six pain points, and every team independently rebuilds the same infrastructure to climb past them. DDx ships that infrastructure once.
./docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md:38:2. **Documents drift silently.** When an upstream document changes, dependent artifacts go stale with no detection or reconciliation. *(DDx: artifact graph + staleness + reconciliation beads.)*
./docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md:48:3. **Methodology is plural.** Different teams, projects, and problem domains demand different workflows — waterfall, agile, kanban, HELIX, ad-hoc. No tool that bakes one in survives the rest. DDx provides primitives (artifacts, runs, beads) that any methodology composes.

./docs/helix/00-discover/references/REF-001-spec-driven.md:32:The workflow begins with an idea—often vague and incomplete. Through iterative dialogue with AI, this idea becomes a comprehensive PRD. The AI asks clarifying questions, identifies edge cases, and helps define precise acceptance criteria. What might take days of meetings and documentation in traditional development happens in hours of focused specification work. This transforms the traditional SDLC—requirements and design become continuous activities rather than discrete phases. This is supportive of a **team process**, that's team reviewed-specifications are expressed and versioned, created in branches, and merged.
./docs/helix/00-discover/references/REF-001-spec-driven.md:174:The commands embody SDD principles by treating specifications as executable artifacts rather than static documents. They transform the specification process from a necessary evil into the driving force of development.

Provides templates, prompts, and workflow scaffolding for treating PRDs and technical plans as the primary, executable artifacts that generate implementation. Cited as a foundational tool/methodology source for DDx and HELIX framing.
./docs/helix/00-discover/references/REF-008-lost-in-the-middle.md:18:Establishes that long-context models do not use their full context uniformly: information placed in the middle is recalled far less reliably than information at the start or end. Cited to justify DDx/HELIX's emphasis on small, governing artifacts and curated context windows over dumping everything into the prompt.
./docs/helix/00-discover/references/REF-028-doc-drift-study.md:17:Empirical evidence that documentation rots quickly when it is decoupled from the build/test loop. Motivates DDx's stance that governing artifacts must be executable or referenced from runtime checks (acceptance criteria, contracts, evidence trails) — not prose to be read once and forgotten.
./docs/helix/00-discover/references/REF-009-context-rot.md:17:Empirical evaluation showing that effective context utilization decays well before models hit their nominal context limit. Reinforces the case for context discipline (governing artifacts, focused beads, retrieval over stuffing) in document-driven agent workflows.

./cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:15:{"id":"corpus-0e60d121f56c","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"bug: /v1/route-targets is not a liveness probe — upstream-services shows 'connected' when LM Studio has no route-target loaded","description":"Discovered 2026-04-15 while routing work to vidar-coder (LM Studio on vidar:1234).\n\n## Two-part bug\n\n### Part 1: bare-name JIT miss (the actual root cause)\n\nInitial probe: `POST /v1/chat/completions {route-target: 'qwen3-coder-next', ...}` → 400 'No route-targets loaded'.\n\nWe assumed LM Studio had no route-target loaded. Wrong. The actual route-target is listed by /v1/route-targets as 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (with the 'qwen/' prefix). LM Studio's just-in-time loading IS enabled and DOES auto-load route-targets on demand — but only when the requested route-target ID matches one in /v1/route-targets exactly. Bare-name 'qwen3-coder-next' didn't match, so JIT couldn't fire, and LM Studio returned the misleading 'No route-targets loaded' error instead of 'unknown route-target'.\n\nVerified by re-probing with 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' — JIT fired, route-target loaded, responded 'OK' in ~60s on cold start.\n\n### Part 2: discovery should be standard practice\n\nThe routing/probe layer should never trust user-supplied bare route-target names against an LM Studio upstream-service. The standard discovery flow should be:\n\n1. GET /v1/route-targets → enumerate the catalog the server is offering\n2. For each catalog ID, optionally probe POST /v1/chat/completions with a 1-token request and a generous timeout (60s+ on cold start, 3s on warm)\n3. Cache the (upstream-service, route-target_id) → inference-ready bool with a short TTL\n4. When user code asks for 'route-target qwen3-coder-next' against this upstream-service, normalize against the discovered catalog: if there's exactly one match by suffix, accept it; if zero or multiple, error with the candidate list\n\nThis converts the 'No route-targets loaded' misleading error into a clear 'requested route-target qwen3-coder-next not in catalog; closest match: qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (or just transparent normalization).\n\n## Impact on cost-tiered routing\n\nThe cost-tiered routing policy (DDx bead ddx-dc231144 tier-based auto-escalation) needs reliable upstream-service readiness signals. Without discovery + normalization:\n- bare-name configs silently fail and force escalation\n- the 'connected (N route-targets)' status is unreliable as a routing input\n- every misrouted attempt burns latency and forces the next tier\n\n## Fix scope\n\n- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to enumerate /v1/route-targets and probe each listed route-target with its exact ID\n- Add a route-target-id normalization layer that maps bare names to catalog IDs by suffix-match (with explicit error on ambiguity)\n- Cache probe results per (upstream-service, route-target) for a short TTL (30s reasonable)\n- Surface the distinction in 'ddx-agent upstream-services' output: http-connected (from /v1/route-targets) vs inference-ready-route-targets (the subset that actually responds)","acceptance":"(1) GET /v1/route-targets is enumerated by the discovery layer; the catalog is the canonical route-target-ID source per upstream-service. (2) Bare route-target names ('qwen3-coder-next') passed by callers are normalized to canonical IDs ('qwen/qwen3-coder-next') by suffix-match against the catalog, with explicit ambiguity errors. (3) upstream-services command shows http-connected and inference-ready-route-targets per upstream-service. (4) Routing policy consumes inference-ready-route-targets, not 'connected (N route-targets)'. (5) Regression test uses a mock LM Studio that lists 'qwen/foo' but rejects 'foo' on chat/completions; discovery normalizes 'foo' → 'qwen/foo' and the probe succeeds.","labels":["kind:bug","area:routing","area:discovery","upstream-service:lmstudio"],"bead":{"id":"agent-40ee5960","title":"bug: /v1/route-targets is not a liveness probe — upstream-services shows 'connected' when LM Studio has no route-target loaded","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"Discovered 2026-04-15 while routing work to vidar-coder (LM Studio on vidar:1234).\n\n## Two-part bug\n\n### Part 1: bare-name JIT miss (the actual root cause)\n\nInitial probe: `POST /v1/chat/completions {route-target: 'qwen3-coder-next', ...}` → 400 'No route-targets loaded'.\n\nWe assumed LM Studio had no route-target loaded. Wrong. The actual route-target is listed by /v1/route-targets as 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (with the 'qwen/' prefix). LM Studio's just-in-time loading IS enabled and DOES auto-load route-targets on demand — but only when the requested route-target ID matches one in /v1/route-targets exactly. Without discovery + normalization:\n- bare-name configs silently fail and force escalation\n- the 'connected (N route-targets)' status is unreliable as a routing input\n- every misrouted attempt burns latency and forces the next tier\n\n## Fix scope\n\n- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to enumerate /v1/route-targets and probe each listed route-target with its exact ID\n- Add a route-target-id normalization layer that maps bare names to catalog IDs by suffix-match (with explicit error on ambiguity)\n- Cache probe results per (upstream-service, route-target) for a short TTL (30s reasonable)\n- Surface the distinction in 'ddx-agent upstream-services' output: http-connected (from /v1/route-targets) vs inference-ready-route-targets (the subset that actually responds)","acceptance":"(1) GET /v1/route-targets is enumerated by the discovery layer; the catalog is the canonical route-target-ID source per upstream-service. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:21:{"id":"corpus-148d699c1390","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. But the contract never defines what ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, and ddx.cost.pricing_ref should look like when all child chat costs are known yet their provenance differs across turns, such as one upstream-service-reported turn plus one configured-cost turn. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only route-targets single-attempt provenance. The current implementation fell into this gap and downgraded a known total to unknown when provenance metadata differed.\nSuggested fix: Amend the root-span cost-rollup section to specify a conformant representation for mixed known-cost provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. Add a contract-focused regression test or checklist entry covering mixed upstream-service-reported/configured sessions.","acceptance":"CONTRACT-001 explicitly defines how the root invoke_agent span represents known session totals when child chat spans have mixed known provenance; the contract no longer requires an impossible or ambiguous ddx.cost.source/pricing_ref combination at the root; conformance tests or checklist coverage include a mixed upstream-service-reported/configured session example.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-7853958b","title":"correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only route-targets single-attempt provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. Add a contract-focused regression test or checklist entry covering mixed upstream-service-reported/configured sessions.","acceptance":"CONTRACT-001 explicitly defines how the root invoke_agent span represents known session totals when child chat spans have mixed known provenance; the contract no longer requires an impossible or ambiguous ddx.cost.source/pricing_ref combination at the root; conformance tests or checklist coverage include a mixed upstream-service-reported/configured session example.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"]}}

Depends on bead A (agent-cccc2df7) for the CachePolicy=\"off\" semantics.\n\nCodex correctly diagnosed the wiring: manifest v4 already has CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM at internal/route-targetcatalog/manifest.go:44-45 (route-target entries) and :139-140 (target entries) with validation. CostAttribution already has CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount at internal/core/agent.go:198-199. Telemetry emission already wires them at internal/core/loop.go:936-940. The only missing piece is: PricingFor at internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go:388 drops the cache fields when projecting to core.Route-targetPricing, and core.Route-targetPricing at internal/core/pricing.go:5 lacks the slots, so loop.go:303 has no source-of-truth to read.\n\nThree coordinated edits:\n\n1. internal/core/pricing.go:5 — add CacheReadPerM, CacheWritePerM float64 to core.Route-targetPricing.\n\n2. internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go:388 (PricingFor) — preserve CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM from the manifest route-target/target onto the returned Route-targetPricing.\n\n3. internal/core/loop.go:303 — extend the configured-cost computation to add cache-read and cache-write costs:\n   computed := input/1e6 * InputPerMTok + output/1e6 * OutputPerMTok\n            + cacheRead/1e6 * CacheReadPerM\n            + cacheWrite/1e6 * CacheWritePerM\n   Source the cache token counts from resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens and CacheWriteTokens. Populate CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = \u0026cacheReadCost and CacheWriteAmount = \u0026cacheWriteCost (slots already there).\n\n4. Honor CachePolicy=\"off\" from bead A: when off, emit CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount as explicit *float64(0.0), NOT nil. nil = runner/upstream-service didn't report; explicit zero = caller asked for no caching. This matches the existing zero-vs-unknown convention from agent-a8cbdb87.\n\n5. Update CONTRACT-003 prose: paragraph in the Cost Attribution / UsageReport section explaining that cache_read and cache_write tokens are priced at the manifest's CostCacheReadPerM/CostCacheWritePerM rates; nil cache amounts mean the runner or upstream-service didn't report cache usage; explicit zero means caching was opted out via CachePolicy=\"off\".\n\nHistorical session-log recalculation is OUT OF SCOPE per codex: existing logs cannot be re-priced reliably from UsageReport alone without changing semantics. Native loop populates cache cost: TestNativeLoopPopulatesCacheCostAttribution drives a fixture catalog through a virtual upstream-service that emits resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens=1000 and CacheWriteTokens=500. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:33:{"id":"corpus-2205629c24fa","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:289\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a upstream-service-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.\nSuggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.","acceptance":"A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed upstream-service-reported/configured cost rollups.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-264e7ea0","title":"correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: loop.go:289\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a upstream-service-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.\nSuggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.","acceptance":"A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed upstream-service-reported/configured cost rollups.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"]}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:43:{"id":"corpus-2cc173f3fffd","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve non-envelope JSON with kind+title","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:375\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: `isPromptEnvelopeProbe` now treats any JSON object with top-level `kind` plus `title` as a DDx prompt envelope candidate. That fixes malformed `{\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"...\"}` envelopes, but it also re-breaks backward compatibility for plain JSON prompts whose `kind` is not the DDx envelope discriminator. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"}' -upstream-service nonexistent` now returns `invalid prompt envelope: kind, id, and prompt are required` instead of passing the JSON through as raw prompt text; the same regression occurs for stdin and `@file` because all three paths call `resolvePrompt` -\u003e `parsePromptInput`. This violates the earlier plain-JSON compatibility requirement while over-matching objects that are not DDx prompt envelopes.\\nSuggested fix: narrow the probe so `title` only contributes to envelope detection when the object is actually a DDx prompt envelope candidate, for example by requiring `kind == \"prompt\"` before `title` triggers validation; add regression coverage proving `{\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"}` stays raw text for inline, stdin, and `@file`, while malformed `{\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"Inspect main\"}` still fails validation.","acceptance":"A plain JSON prompt like {\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"} remains raw prompt text for -p, stdin, and @file inputs, while malformed DDx prompt envelopes like {\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"Inspect main\"} still fail with an invalid prompt envelope error; regression tests cover both behaviors.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-5e455139","title":"correctness: preserve non-envelope JSON with kind+title","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:375\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: `isPromptEnvelopeProbe` now treats any JSON object with top-level `kind` plus `title` as a DDx prompt envelope candidate. That fixes malformed `{\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"...\"}` envelopes, but it also re-breaks backward compatibility for plain JSON prompts whose `kind` is not the DDx envelope discriminator. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"}' -upstream-service nonexistent` now returns `invalid prompt envelope: kind, id, and prompt are required` instead of passing the JSON through as raw prompt text; the same regression occurs for stdin and `@file` because all three paths call `resolvePrompt` -\u003e `parsePromptInput`. This violates the earlier plain-JSON compatibility requirement while over-matching objects that are not DDx prompt envelopes.\\nSuggested fix: narrow the probe so `title` only contributes to envelope detection when the object is actually a DDx prompt envelope candidate, for example by requiring `kind == \"prompt\"` before `title` triggers validation; add regression coverage proving `{\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"}` stays raw text for inline, stdin, and `@file`, while malformed `{\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"Inspect main\"}` still fails validation.","acceptance":"A plain JSON prompt like {\"kind\":\"note\",\"title\":\"hello\"} remains raw prompt text for -p, stdin, and @file inputs, while malformed DDx prompt envelopes like {\"kind\":\"prompt\",\"title\":\"Inspect main\"} still fail with an invalid prompt envelope error; regression tests cover both behaviors.","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"]}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:62:{"id":"corpus-42009f026676","source_repo":"agent","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed focused bead with small acceptance surface","title":"correctness: anthropic streaming usage loses input tokens across deltas","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 51f778b.\nFile: upstream-service/anthropic/anthropic.go:236, upstream-service/anthropic/anthropic.go:275, stream_consume.go:84\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Anthropic streaming emits input tokens on the initial message_start delta and output/cache tokens on later message_delta events, but consumeStream replaces resp.Usage on each delta instead of accumulating it. In the default streaming path, the final Response therefore drops the earlier input token counts and retains only the last usage fragment. Anthropic's streaming code also never sets Total, so even if the fragments were merged the run-level total token count would remain zero. This leaves llm.response, session.end, and any cost/accounting derived from streaming Anthropic responses inconsistent with the non-streaming upstream-service path.\nSuggested fix: Accumulate streaming usage fragments in consumeStream instead of overwriting them, and have the Anthropic streaming path emit enough data to compute a correct final TokenUsage.Total, or emit the full merged usage on the terminal delta. Add a regression test that simulates Anthropic-style split usage deltas and asserts the final Response and emitted response event preserve input, output, cache, and total token counts.","acceptance":"For Anthropic streaming runs, token usage from message_start and message_delta is merged into the final Response and llm.response event; Input, Output, CacheRead, and CacheWrite are preserved; Total equals Input+Output; a regression test covers split usage deltas","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"],"bead":{"id":"hx-24b844fb","title":"correctness: anthropic streaming usage loses input tokens across deltas","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 51f778b.\nFile: upstream-service/anthropic/anthropic.go:236, upstream-service/anthropic/anthropic.go:275, stream_consume.go:84\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Anthropic streaming emits input tokens on the initial message_start delta and output/cache tokens on later message_delta events, but consumeStream replaces resp.Usage on each delta instead of accumulating it. In the default streaming path, the final Response therefore drops the earlier input token counts and retains only the last usage fragment. Anthropic's streaming code also never sets Total, so even if the fragments were merged the run-level total token count would remain zero. This leaves llm.response, session.end, and any cost/accounting derived from streaming Anthropic responses inconsistent with the non-streaming upstream-service path.\nSuggested fix: Accumulate streaming usage fragments in consumeStream instead of overwriting them, and have the Anthropic streaming path emit enough data to compute a correct final TokenUsage.Total, or emit the full merged usage on the terminal delta. Add a regression test that simulates Anthropic-style split usage deltas and asserts the final Response and emitted response event preserve input, output, cache, and total token counts.","acceptance":"For Anthropic streaming runs, token usage from message_start and message_delta is merged into the final Response and llm.response event; Input, Output, CacheRead, and CacheWrite are preserved; Total equals Input+Output; a regression test covers split usage deltas","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding"]}}

./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:83:{"id":"corpus-564806177b8d","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve doctor audit for legacy untyped plugin installs","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/doctor.go:454\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: `checkInstalledPlugins` now skips any installed entry whose `type` is empty unless the package name exists in `BuiltinRegistry()`. That fixes resource false-positives, but it also suppresses `ddx doctor --plugins` for legacy or third-party plugin installs whose older `installed.yaml` rows lack `type`. The project design explicitly keeps a builtin-registry fallback for legacy packages and expects old packages to remain auditable during the migration period, so this filter drops coverage for a supported compatibility path.\nSuggested fix: When `entry.Type` is empty, infer plugin-like roots from the recorded files or installed root path instead of skipping outright, and add a regression test for an untyped installed plugin entry that still produces manifest/structure findings.","acceptance":"Given an installed plugin entry with an empty type field and plugin-root files, when ddx doctor --plugins runs, then it still audits that entry and reports manifest or structure problems instead of silently skipping it","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"hx-9ebbd9de","title":"correctness: preserve doctor audit for legacy untyped plugin installs","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/doctor.go:454\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: `checkInstalledPlugins` now skips any installed entry whose `type` is empty unless the package name exists in `BuiltinRegistry()`. That fixes resource false-positives, but it also suppresses `ddx doctor --plugins` for legacy or third-party plugin installs whose older `installed.yaml` rows lack `type`. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:125:{"id":"corpus-84b3a485c4f4","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. FEAT-004 requires unknown fields to be preserved on read/write operations, and FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as durable replay/audit provenance. Losing that field on an unrelated closed bead silently corrupts the replay baseline for the SD-018 verification task even though this commit did not govern that bead. Suggested fix: repair hx-ddx-agent-target-verify so its closing_commit_sha is restored to 2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and add/adjust tracker update coverage so finalizing one bead cannot clobber preserved fields on unrelated rows.","acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-ddx-agent-target-verify --json reports closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and regression coverage proves updating or closing hx-9c1a1558 leaves unrelated bead fields unchanged","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:agent","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"hx-a51dd27a","title":"correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads","status":"closed","issue_type":"task","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. FEAT-004 requires unknown fields to be preserved on read/write operations, and FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as durable replay/audit provenance. Losing that field on an unrelated closed bead silently corrupts the replay baseline for the SD-018 verification task even though this commit did not govern that bead. Suggested fix: repair hx-ddx-agent-target-verify so its closing_commit_sha is restored to 2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and add/adjust tracker update coverage so finalizing one bead cannot clobber preserved fields on unrelated rows.","acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-ddx-agent-target-verify --json reports closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and regression coverage proves updating or closing hx-9c1a1558 leaves unrelated bead fields unchanged","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:agent","area:cli"]}}

self.collection_exists_in_namespace(collection, namespace)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Per-tenant PostgreSQL provisioning ───────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\\n///\\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n        return next.run(request).await;\\n    };\\n\\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n    let database = database.to_string();\\n    let method = request.method().clone();\\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n    match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n        Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n            let mut request = request;\\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n            next.run(request).await\\n        }\\n        Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n    state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n    headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n    method: \u0026Method,\\n    tenant: \u0026str,\\n    database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs();\\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n    // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n    }\\n    // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n    if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n        .jti\\n        .parse()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n    }\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        if storage\\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n        {\\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n            drop(storage);\\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n    let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n    let user = {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n    };\\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n    let granted_db = claims\\n        .grants\\n        .find_database(database)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n        Op::Read\\n    } else {\\n        Op::Write\\n    };\\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n        user_id,\\n        tenant_id,\\n        grants: claims.grants,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n    let auth_header = headers\\n        .get(http::header::AUTHORIZATION)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n\\n    let auth_str = auth_header\\n        .to_str()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n\\n    auth_str\\n        .strip_prefix(\\\"Bearer \\\")\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n}\\n\\n/// Extract `(tenant, database)` from a data-plane URL path.\\n///\\n/// Returns `Some((tenant, database))` for paths matching\\n/// `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/…`, `None` otherwise.\\n/// Non-data-plane paths skip the middleware entirely.\\npub fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e {\\n    let path = path.strip_prefix('/')?;\\n    let mut parts = path.splitn(5, '/');\\n    match (parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next(), parts.next()) {\\n        (Some(\\\"tenants\\\"), Some(tenant), Some(\\\"databases\\\"), Some(rest))\\n            if !tenant.is_empty() \u0026\u0026 !rest.is_empty() =\u003e\\n        {\\n            let database = rest.split('/').next()?;\\n            if database.is_empty() {\\n                None\\n            } else {\\n                Some((tenant, database))\\n            }\\n        }\\n        _ =\u003e None,\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthError → axum Response ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Newtype wrapper that lets us implement `IntoResponse` for `AuthError` in\\n/// this crate (orphan-rule: neither `IntoResponse` nor `AuthError` is local).\\npub struct AuthErrorResponse(pub AuthError);\\n\\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthErrorResponse {\\n    fn into_response(self) -\u003e Response {\\n        let status = StatusCode::from_u16(self.0.status_code())\\n            .unwrap_or(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR);\\n        let body = json!({\\n            \\\"error\\\": {\\n                \\\"code\\\": self.0.error_code(),\\n                \\\"message\\\": self.0.to_string()\\n            }\\n        });\\n        (status, axum::Json(body)).into_response()\\n GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\n//!\\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\nconst ISSUER: \u0026str = \\\"integration-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants → 200 ──────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let token = valid_token(\u0026state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: \\\"different-tenant\\\".to_string(), // URL says \\\"acme\\\"\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\\n    };\\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(\u0026claims).unwrap();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"credential_wrong_tenant\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: \\\"invoices\\\".to_string(), // URL says \\\"orders\\\"\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    };\\n    let token = directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. ---
ddx:
  id: TD-029
  depends_on:
    - TD-026
  status: draft
---
# Technical Design: Artifact Full-Text Search & Facet Contract

## Status

Draft. Implements the URL/key facet contract for the artifacts list view
(Story 6) alongside the categorization axes published by Story 5
(`grouping.ts`). Codifies the GraphQL `Query.artifacts` filter surface
so server, client, and grouping logic agree on a single set of
reserved keys.

## Motivation

The artifacts list page (`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts/`)
is the primary entry point for browsing DDx-tracked documents and
sidecar artifacts. Story 5 added grouping axes (`folder`, `prefix`,
`mediaType`, …); Story 6 adds the corresponding filter axes. To keep
the URL stable across navigations and bookmarks, and to keep the
server resolver, frontend chip components, and the Story 5 grouping
helpers from drifting on naming, this TD pins the facet contract.

## Reserved URL keys

The following query parameters are reserved on the artifacts list URL.
Unknown keys are preserved verbatim by `urlState.writeState` and never
treated as facets.

| Key         | Cardinality | Type      | Source of truth | Purpose |
|-------------|-------------|-----------|-----------------|---------|
| `q`         | single      | string    | resolver: title∪path substring (case-insensitive) | Full-text search |
| `mediaType` | single      | string    | exact match or `<top>/*` wildcard | Media-type narrowing |
| `staleness` | single      | enum (`fresh`/`stale`/`missing`) | resolver: doc graph + sidecar source-hash check | Surface drift |
| `phase`     | single      | string    | path prefix match against `docs/helix/<NN-slug>/` | HELIX phase narrowing |
| `prefix`    | multi (CSV) | string[]  | id-prefix segment (`ADR`/`SD`/`FEAT`/`US`/`RSCH`/`PRD`/…) | Document-class narrowing |
| `sort`      | single      | enum (`PATH`/`TITLE`/`MODIFIED`/`DEPS_COUNT`/`ID`) | resolver | Ordering |

`groupBy` (`folder`/`prefix`/`mediaType`) is a **categorization axis**,
not a filter. It travels in the same URL but does not narrow results —
Story 5 grouping organizes whatever set the filters above leave behind.

The CSV encoding for `prefix` is intentional: it keeps the URL short,
keeps round-tripping symmetric (`writeState` joins on `,`,
`readState` splits and trims), and avoids URLSearchParams' implicit
multi-key semantics that not every client serializer agrees on.

## Source-of-truth definitions for new axes

### Phase axis (single-value)

A document or sidecar artifact's phase is derived from its repo-relative
path. The resolver matches when the path falls under `docs/helix/<DIR>/`
where `<DIR>` is either:

- the exact phase value (e.g. `phase=01-frame` matches
  `docs/helix/01-frame/PRD.md`), or
- a numeric prefix matching the leading `NN-` segment of `<DIR>`
  (e.g. `phase=01` also matches `01-frame/`, `01-foo/`, …).

The frontend chip set publishes the canonical phases (`01-frame`,
`02-design`, `03-test`, `04-build`, `05-deploy`, `06-iterate`).
Artifacts outside `docs/helix/` (sidecar plugins, ad-hoc docs) never
match a phase filter — they are excluded when `phase` is set.

### Prefix axis (multi-value, OR)

Every artifact ID has an implicit class prefix — the segment before
the first `-` in the bare ID (after stripping the namespace prefix
`doc:` or `sidecar:`). Examples:

- `doc:ADR-001-foo` → prefix `ADR`
- `doc:FEAT-022` → prefix `FEAT`
- `sidecar:diagram-001` → prefix `DIAGRAM`

The filter is multi-value with OR semantics: `prefix=ADR,FEAT` matches
any artifact whose prefix segment is `ADR` *or* `FEAT`. Prefix matching
is case-insensitive and is performed on the resolver after every other
filter has run, so `prefix` composes additively with `q`, `mediaType`,
`staleness`, and `phase`.

The frontend's prefix chip set publishes the common HELIX classes
(`ADR`, `SD`, `FEAT`, `US`, `RSCH`, `PRD`); other prefixes remain
filterable through direct URL manipulation but are not surfaced as
chips by default.

## Determinism

Cursor pagination requires a total order. The resolver enforces the
existing `(sortKey, id)` lexicographic tie-break (see
`sortArtifacts` in `resolver_artifacts.go`) for every supported
`sort` value, so the new `phase` and `prefix` filters do not change
ordering — they only narrow the set the sort runs over.

## Story 5 / Story 6 coordination

Story 5 grouping consumes the same axis names defined here. The
shared axis labels MUST not be renamed in either layer without an
update to this TD:

- "phase" — the URL key, the resolver argument, and the
  Story 5 grouping derivation key all agree.
- "prefix" — `urlState.ts` exposes `prefix: string[]`,
  `grouping.ts` exposes `prefixOf(...)` for the matching axis.

A filter narrows the set; grouping organizes the remaining set.

## Search semantics

The `q` filter is a single-pass case-insensitive substring scan with the
following per-artifact field precedence. The first field that matches
is enough to admit the artifact; later fields are not consulted, so the
ordering controls which artifacts surface earliest when scoring is
introduced (Story 6 B4b) and keeps the resolver O(N) on cold cache:

1. `title`
2. `path`
3. `description` (sidecar `ddx.description` or doc subtitle if present)
4. `frontmatter` (raw stringified `ddxFrontmatter` JSON — covers
   `id`, `depends_on`, sidecar `generated_by`, etc.)
5. `body` (file contents — only consulted under the rules below)

Until B4b lands, the resolver only consults fields 1–2 (`title`, `path`);
fields 3–5 are reserved by this contract so that adding them later does
not change ordering for existing matches.

### Body search rules

Body content is only consulted when fields 1–4 did not match. To keep
the scan bounded:

- **Per-file size cap:** `256 KiB` (262 144 bytes). Files larger than the
  cap are read up to the cap only; the remainder is not searched. The
  cap is a constant in `resolver_artifacts.go`
  (`searchBodySizeCapBytes`) so callers can audit the limit without
  reading the resolver.
- **Binary-file skip:** files are classified as binary and skipped when
  either:
  - the path's extension is in the binary allowlist
    (`.png .jpg .jpeg .gif .webp .ico .pdf .zip .tar .gz .bz2 .xz .7z
     .mp3 .mp4 .mov .avi .webm .wasm .so .dylib .dll .exe .bin .o .a
     .class .jar .ttf .otf .woff .woff2 .excalidraw.png`), or
  - the first 512 bytes of the file contain a NUL byte (`0x00`) — a
    cheap content sniff that catches misnamed binaries while preserving
    UTF-8 text.

  SVG (`.svg`) and Excalidraw JSON (`.excalidraw`,
  `.excalidraw.json`) are deliberately treated as **text** because their
  content-search value (titles, comments, label text) outweighs the
  scan cost.

### Deterministic ordering on score ties

When B4b introduces score-based ranking (e.g. exact-token > prefix >
substring), ties on the score MUST resolve via the existing
`(sortKey, id)` lexicographic tie-break used by `sortArtifacts` in
`resolver_artifacts.go`. The tie-break is a hard guarantee: two
artifacts with identical scores ordered identically across pages, on
every machine, every run. This is what cursor pagination depends on,
and it is non-negotiable.

### Upgrade trigger to bleve / sqlite-FTS

The in-process linear scan is intentionally simple. We upgrade to a
real index (bleve, or sqlite-FTS5) when **any** of the following
sustained-state thresholds is crossed:

- Steady-state corpus exceeds **10 000 artifacts** in a single project
  (≈20× the benchmark fixture below).
- p95 latency for a `q` query at the project's actual corpus size
  exceeds **150 ms** for two consecutive weekly measurements on the
  reference dev hardware.
- Total scanned bytes per `q` query exceeds **128 MiB** with the size
  cap and binary skip already applied (i.e. the corpus itself is
  text-heavy enough that the cap is not enough).

Crossing one threshold is sufficient — we do not wait for two. The
bleve/FTS migration lives behind a feature flag so projects can stay
on the linear scan if they prefer the simpler dependency footprint.

### Performance budget

The reference benchmark (`BenchmarkArtifactsSearch_500Fixture` in
`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts_bench_test.go`) builds
a 500-sidecar fixture with a realistic mix:

| Class               | Count | Size range  |
|---------------------|-------|-------------|
| Small markdown      | 200   | 1–8 KiB     |
| Medium markdown     | 100   | 8–64 KiB    |
| Large markdown      | 50    | 64–256 KiB  |
| Oversize markdown   | 25    | 1–4 MiB     |
| PNG (binary)        | 75    | 4–32 KiB    |
| Tarball (binary)    | 50    | 32–256 KiB  |

**p95 budget for end-to-end `q`-only search at this fixture size:
200 ms** on the reference dev hardware (Linux arm64 / Apple M-series,
Go 1.22+, warm filesystem cache). The benchmark prints observed p50,
p95, and p99 microseconds per op so regressions are visible in CI.

The budget is dominated by `collectArtifacts`, which today re-walks
`.ddx/plugins/` and re-parses every `.ddx.yaml` on each call. The
substring scan itself is sub-millisecond at 500 entries. The headroom
above pure search is intentional — it preserves the linear-scan
design through B4b (which adds body search, also bounded by the
256 KiB / binary-skip rules). When B4b lands, the budget is
re-verified against the same fixture and tightened if the body scan
fits underneath.

Measured baseline at the time of writing
(`go test -bench=BenchmarkArtifactsSearch_500Fixture -run=^$ -benchtime=3x`,
Linux arm64, in-CI runner): p95 ≈ 148 ms — within the 200 ms budget.

If the measured p95 deviates by more than ±50% from the budget on a
new platform, document the delta in the benchmark output and update
this section rather than silently moving the budget.

## Implementation pointers

- Schema: `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql` →
  `Query.artifacts(... phase: String, prefix: [String!])`.
- Resolver: `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go` →
  `matchesPhase`, `idPrefixSegment`.
- URL state: `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.ts`
  (`readState`/`writeState` for `phase`, `prefix`).
- Chips: `.../artifacts/+page.svelte` (`PHASE_OPTIONS`,
  `PREFIX_OPTIONS`).
- Tests: `cli/internal/server/graphql/artifacts_test.go`
  (`TestArtifacts_PhaseFilter`, `TestArtifacts_PrefixFilter`,
  `TestArtifacts_PhasePrefixComposeWithOtherFilters`);
  `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.test.ts`.
- Benchmark:
  `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts_bench_test.go`
  (`BenchmarkArtifactsSearch_500Fixture`).
